heading: Preference right of selection granted certain Western States; bona fide settlers
label: 861 Preference right of selection granted certain Western States; bona fide settlers
text: The States of North Dakota, South Dakota, Montana, Idaho, and Washington shall have a preference right over any person or corporation to select lands subject to entry by said States by the Act of Congress approved , for a period of sixty days after lands have been surveyed and duly declared to be subject to selection and entry under the general land laws of the United States.February 22, 1889Such preference right shall not accrue against bona fide homestead or preemption settlers on any of said lands at the date of filing of the plat of survey of any township in any local land office of said States.
notes: References in TextAct , referred to in text, is , . Provisions relating to admission of the enumerated States into the Union are not classified to the Code.February 22, 1889act Feb. 22, 1889, ch. 18025 Stat. 676
